title: Ezra and the Scared Little Dragon
synopsis: When young Ezra finds a tiny, scared dragon hiding in his garden, he learns that being brave means helping others feel safe. Through gentle kindness and friendship, Ezra helps the little dragon overcome his fears and find his way home to his dragon family.

pages.text: Ezra loved playing in his backyard garden. He had dark curly hair and wore his favorite red shirt with a smiling sun on it. Today felt special, like magic was in the air!

illustration.description: A cheerful young Black boy with dark curly hair wearing a bright red shirt with a yellow sun design stands in a colorful garden. The garden has vibrant flowers in purple, pink, and yellow, with green leafy plants. The background shows a blue sky with fluffy white clouds. The art style is bright cartoon with rounded, friendly shapes and a slightly textured background like construction paper.
illustration.alt: Ezra, a young Black boy, standing happily in a colorful garden

pages.text: Behind the big sunflowers, Ezra heard a tiny sound. "Sniff, sniff." Someone was crying! Ezra felt curious and a little worried. Who could be sad in his happy garden?

illustration.description: Ezra crouching down near tall yellow sunflowers, his face showing concern and curiosity. His hand is cupped to his ear as he listens to soft crying sounds. The sunflowers tower above him with their bright yellow petals and dark centers. Small tears or sound waves are visible coming from behind the flowers. The scene uses warm yellows and greens with a textured paper-like background.
illustration.alt: Ezra listening carefully to crying sounds coming from behind sunflowers

pages.text: Ezra peeked behind the sunflowers and gasped! There was a tiny purple dragon, no bigger than a kitten. The little dragon was shaking and looked very scared. His golden eyes were full of tears.

illustration.description: A small, adorable purple dragon with golden eyes sitting among green leaves, looking frightened with tear drops on his cheeks. The dragon has tiny wings, a long tail, and gentle features. Ezra's face is visible at the edge of the image, showing surprise and concern. The dragon is curled up small, making himself look even tinier. Bright primary colors with soft shadows and rounded shapes.
illustration.alt: A tiny scared purple dragon with golden eyes crying behind the sunflowers

pages.text: "Hello, little dragon," Ezra said softly. "I'm Ezra. Why are you sad?" The dragon looked up with his big golden eyes. "I'm lost," he whispered. "I can't find my mama and papa. I'm too scared to fly home."

illustration.description: Ezra kneeling down at the dragon's level, speaking gently with a kind expression. The purple dragon is looking up at him with large, sad golden eyes. Both characters are surrounded by green garden plants and colorful flowers. Ezra's body language is open and welcoming, while the dragon still looks small and frightened but slightly hopeful. Warm, comforting colors dominate the scene.
illustration.alt: Ezra talking gently to the scared little purple dragon

pages.text: Ezra felt brave and wanted to help. "Don't be scared," he said. "I'll help you find your family. What's your name?" The little dragon wiped his eyes. "I'm Pip," he said. "Thank you for being kind, Ezra."

illustration.description: Ezra reaching out his hand toward Pip the dragon in a gesture of friendship. Pip is wiping his eyes with a tiny claw but starting to smile. The background shows the garden bathed in warm afternoon sunlight. Both characters look more hopeful now, with brighter expressions. The composition shows them getting closer together, suggesting the beginning of friendship.
illustration.alt: Ezra offering friendship to Pip the dragon, who is starting to feel better

pages.text: Ezra had an idea! "Pip, maybe if you sit on my shoulder, you'll feel brave enough to look for your family from up high." Pip felt happy that Ezra wanted to help him. Slowly, he climbed onto Ezra's shoulder.

illustration.description: Pip the small purple dragon carefully climbing onto Ezra's shoulder, both characters showing cautious optimism. Ezra has a gentle, encouraging smile and is standing still to help Pip feel safe. Pip's expression shows he's still nervous but trusting. The garden background is bright and welcoming. The illustration emphasizes the size difference between the tiny dragon and the boy.
illustration.alt: Pip climbing onto Ezra's shoulder while both look hopeful

pages.text: From Ezra's shoulder, Pip could see over the garden wall. "Look!" Pip pointed with his tiny claw. "I can see the big oak tree where I live!" Pip felt excited and brave now. He wasn't scared anymore because Ezra was his friend.

illustration.description: Ezra standing tall with Pip perched confidently on his shoulder, both looking toward a large oak tree visible in the distance. Pip is pointing excitedly with his tiny claw, and his expression shows joy and relief. The garden wall is visible, and beyond it is a magnificent oak tree with a thick trunk and spreading branches. Both characters look happy and triumphant.
illustration.alt: Ezra and Pip spotting the oak tree where Pip's family lives

pages.text: Two larger dragons flew down from the oak tree - one pink and one blue. "Pip!" they called happily. "Mama! Papa!" Pip cried with joy. He flew into their arms for the biggest dragon hug ever. Ezra felt so happy watching the family reunion.

illustration.description: A joyful reunion scene with Pip flying toward two larger dragons - a pink mother dragon and a blue father dragon - who are flying down from the oak tree. All three dragons are embracing in a group hug with expressions of pure happiness. Ezra stands nearby watching with a big smile, feeling proud and happy. The scene is filled with warm colors and shows love and family connection.
illustration.alt: Pip reuniting with his dragon parents in a happy family hug

pages.text: "Thank you, Ezra," said Pip's mama. "You were very brave and kind to help our little one." Pip flew over and hugged Ezra's cheek. "You're my best friend," Pip said. "I'll visit you every day!" Ezra felt proud and happy. He had helped his scared friend feel brave.

illustration.description: Pip giving Ezra a gentle hug on his cheek while Pip's parents look on gratefully in the background. Ezra has a huge smile showing pride and happiness. The dragon family looks warm and thankful. The setting shows the garden with the oak tree nearby, suggesting this is now a special place of friendship. The overall mood is celebratory and heartwarming with bright, cheerful colors.
illustration.alt: Pip hugging Ezra while his grateful parents watch, everyone looking happy

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Ezra feel when he wanted to help Pip?
questions.explanation: Ezra felt brave when he decided to help the scared little dragon find his family.

questions.question: Why was Pip crying in the garden?
questions.explanation: Pip was crying because he was lost and couldn't find his mama and papa dragon.

questions.question: How did Ezra help Pip see his family's tree?
questions.explanation: Ezra let Pip sit on his shoulder so the little dragon could see over the garden wall to spot his family's oak tree.

questions.question: How did Pip feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Pip felt happy at the end because he found his family and made a new friend in Ezra.

sentences.text: Ezra found a tiny _____ dragon hiding in his garden.
Pip was _____ because he couldn't find his family.
Ezra felt _____ when he decided to help Pip.
Pip felt _____ when he saw his mama and papa.
